issue.title: [$250] Expense - Unable to sort expenses by Attendees and Per attendee
issue.body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.4.19-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** N/A - new feature, doesn't exist in prod **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/94124 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** sdjnoisdjdosijsonssjo@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Mac 26.5 / Chrome **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: Precondition: - Enable Rules. - Enable Attendee tracking in Rules settings. 1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to workspace chat. 3. Create two expenses. 4. Go to Spend > Expenses. 5. Click Display > Edit columns. 6. Select Attendees and Per attendee > Save. 7. Click Attendees and Per attendee. → Expenses can be sorted by Attendees and Per attendee on Spend > Expenses. 8. Go to Inbox. 9. Open the report. 10. Click Columns. 11 Select Attendees and Per attendee > Save. 12. Click Attendees and Per attendee. ## Expected Result: Expenses can be sorted by Attendees and Per attendee in expense report (similar to Spend > Expenses). ## Actual Result: Expenses cannot be sorted by Attendees and Per attendee in expense report (similar to Spend > Expenses). ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5c4fe6b6-ee6e-4a67-b7fa-dcdcbe00392a [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22) > **Applause Internal Information** > Exported by: Mita Rachim > Bug ID: 7190137

fix_pr.title: Make Attendees and Per attendee columns sortable in expense report view
fix_pr.body_preview:
### Explanation of Change

The report-view transactions table and the Search (Spend → Expenses) table use two independent sorting paths. PR #94124 added the **Attendees** and **Per attendee** columns to the single-report column picker, but the report-view sort path in `src/libs/ReportUtils.ts` was never updated, so these columns rendered as non-sortable in an expense report even though they are sortable on Search.

This PR brings the report-view sort path to parity with Search:

- Adds `ATTENDEES` and `TOTAL_PER_ATTENDEE` to the `sortableColumnNames` allow-list (read by `isSortableColumnName`, which `MoneyRequestReportTableHeader` uses to decide whether a header is sortable).
- Adds matching `case`s to `getTransactionSortValue`, mirroring the Search implementation in `SearchUIUtils.ts`: **Attendees** sorts by attendee count, **Per attendee** sorts by `amount / attendeeCount` (0 when there are no attendees).
- Also adds `POSTED` to the report-view sort path (sortable on Search but previously not in the report view), with unit tests.
- Adds unit tests covering the new sort values and the updated sortable-column allow-list.

### Fixed Issues

$ https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/94507
PROPOSAL: https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/94507#issuecomment-4793665527

### Tests

Preconditions:
* Large screen Web only.
* Create a workspace
* Enable Rules.
* Enable Attendee tracking in Rules settings.


1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com.
2. Open a workspace chat.
3. Create two expenses: one with 1 attendee and one with 2 attendees.
4. Open the report.
5. Click Columns.
6. Select Attendees and Per attendee, then click Save.
7. Click the Attendees column header.
8. Click the Per attendee column header.
9. Verify that expenses can be sorted by both Attendees and Per attendee

expected_triage.evaluation_focus: Sorting bug with clear repro and focused fix files.

fix_pr.title: [Submit] Show "Needs approval" for Submit approvers in Search/Spend
fix_pr.body_preview:
### Explanation of Change

A Submit workspace approver couldn't find reports awaiting their approval in the Search/Spend **"Needs approval"** section, even when a report was submitted directly to them.

The approve suggested search (`SearchUIUtils.getSuggestedSearchesVisibility`) gated the **APPROVE** key on `isPaidGroupPolicy`, which excludes Submit (`submit2026`). So "Needs approval" never surfaced for a Submit approver, even though the to-do layer (`isApproveAction`) already treats Submit approvals as valid.

This PR aligns the Search visibility with `isApproveAction`:
- `isEligibleForApproveSuggestion` treats a Submit workspace as approval-enabled regardless of `approvalMode`.
- The gate widens from `isPaidGroupPolicy` to `isGroupPolicy`, so a Submit **approver** sees "Needs approval" while a plain submitter does not. Collect/Control/Personal are unchanged (`isGroupPolicy` is a strict superset).

 
 
### Fixed Issues

$ https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/94187
PROPOSAL:

### Tests

1. As User A, create a **Submit** workspace.
2. Create an expense and submit it to User B (the approver).
3. Sign in as User B and open **Search → Reports** (Spend): verify the **"Needs approval"** section appears and lists the submitted report.
 
 
### Offline tests

Suggested-search visibility is derived from already-synced Onyx data, so it behaves identically offline. Same as Tests.

### QA Steps

Same as Tests (use an adhoc build to sign in as the approver, User B).

- [X] Verify that no errors appear in the JS console

### PR Author Checklist

- [X] I linked the correct issue in the `### Fixed Issues` section above
- [X] I wrote clear testing steps that cover the changes made in this PR
    - [X] I added steps for local testing in the `Tests

Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to workspace chat. 3. Create two expenses without category. 4. Go to Home. 5. Click Review 2 expenses. 6. Click Category and select any category. 7. Close the report view. 8. Click Review 1 expense. ## Expected Result: Expense RHP with missing category will open. ## Actual Result: Expense report RHP opens, showing both expenses with and without violation. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c450f0c8-d43e-470c-b916-0d9d68836dd3 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22) > **Applause Internal Information** > Exported by: Julia Ponikarchuk > Bug ID: 7196900

fix_pr.title: [CP Staging] Fix: open flagged expense thread when its report holds multiple transactions
fix_pr.body_preview:
### Explanation of Change

The "Review X expenses" row on Home opened the **whole expense report** when a single flagged expense lived inside a report that still held other (unflagged) transactions, instead of opening the RHP for the flagged expense.

The handler in `useReviewFlaggedExpenses` decided between "open the report directly" and "open the single transaction thread" with:

```ts
if (flaggedExpenses.length === 1 && isMoneyRequestReport(firstFlaggedReport)) { ... }
```

`isMoneyRequestReport` only checks the report **type** (`isIOUReport || isExpenseReport`), which is always `true` for the OPEN expense reports this hook scans. So `flaggedExpenses.length === 1` alone drove the branch, and the "open the report directly" shortcut — intended only for genuine one-transaction reports — fired even when the report contained multiple expenses.

The fix swaps the type check for the transaction-count check that expresses the real intent, [`isOneTransactionReport`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/989d3ea60e83ab775c6f2a9b62107276b9590ced/src/libs/ReportUtils.ts#L2747) (`report?.transactionCount === 1`):

```ts
if (flaggedExpenses.length === 1 && isOneTransactionReport(firstFlaggedReport)) { ... }
```

Now a lone flagged expense inside a multi-transaction report falls through to `navigateToTransactionThread`, opening the RHP for that single flagged expense (matching the Expected Result). The "open the report directly" shortcut stays reserved for reports that genuinely hold only one expense.

Added unit coverage in `tests/unit/hooks/useReviewFlaggedExpenses.test.ts` (lone flagged expense in a 2-transaction report → opens the thread) and UI coverage in `tests/ui/ForYouSectionTest.tsx` for both the multi-transaction (→ thread) and single-transaction (→ report directly) path

expected_triage.evaluation_focus: Navigation bug in Home review flow with clear repro and small focused fix.

issue.title: LHN - Marking an unread message as unread does not mark the conversation as unread
issue.body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel!
___

**Version Number:** 9.4.26-0
**Reproducible in staging?:** Yes
**Reproducible in production?:** No
**If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/62923718/folder/13176910/41237480/1534027983?sort=%2Bidentifier
**Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** n/n
**Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team
**Bug source:** Regression TC Execution
**Device used:** Mac 15.4.1 Safari
**App Component:** Left Hand Navigation (LHN)

## Action Performed:
1. Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com
2. Navigate to a unread conversation with some history
3. Mark a new message as unread
4. Navigate away from the conversation

## Expected Result:
The conversation is marked as unread
When navigating back to the conversation, the new message marker is still displayed on top of the message

## Actual Result:
Marking an unread message as unread does not mark the conversation as unread, the new message marker is not displayed on top of the message

## Workaround:
Unknown

## Platforms:
- [ ] Android: App
- [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome
- [ ] iOS: App
- [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari
- [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome
- [ ] Windows: Chrome
- [x] MacOS: Chrome Safari

## Screenshots/Videos
